summary:
## Overview
Adds the orchestration launch modal — a one-time onboarding modal announcing Oz multi-agent features. The implementation follows the established one-time modal pattern (feature flag, settings-backed dismissed state, `OneTimeModalModel` trigger logic, workspace view integration). Badge styling is also updated consistently across three launch modals (`openwarp_launch_modal`, `welcome_banner`, and this new modal), and a `MAGENTA` design token is added to the color system.

Visual evidence is present (screenshot in the PR description). Placeholder URLs and the hero image are acknowledged as temporary.

## Concerns
- `LEARN_MORE_URL` uses `http://` rather than `https://`. Low risk since it's a placeholder, but the constant should use HTTPS before the final URL is wired in.
- `warp_core::ui::color::MAGENTA` is referenced via a full path qualifier in `orchestration_pill_bar.rs`, contrary to repo conventions which prefer imports over long path qualifiers.
- The new settings field is missing the explanatory comment block that the analogous fields (`did_check_to_trigger_agents_3_launch_modal`, `did_check_to_trigger_oz_launch_modal`) carry.

## Verdict
Found: 0 critical, 0 important, 3 nits

**Approve with nits**

comments.body:
🧹 [NIT] Uses `http://` instead of `https://`. Fine for now since this is a placeholder, but set it to HTTPS before swapping in the real URL to avoid a mixed-content or redirect round-trip.

```suggestion
const LEARN_MORE_URL: &str = "https://warp.dev/placeholder-launch-blog-link";
```

🧹 [NIT] `warp_core::ui::color::MAGENTA` uses a long path qualifier. Per repo conventions, prefer an import. `coloru_with_opacity` is already imported from the same module on line 13 — add `MAGENTA` there.

```suggestion
    let text_color = MAGENTA;
```

🧹 [NIT] Add `MAGENTA` to this import to accompany the suggestion on line 479.

```suggestion
use warp_core::ui::color::{coloru_with_opacity, MAGENTA};
```
